name: Friendship Cake and Starter
recipeIngredient: 1 cup sliced canned peaches, including juice
recipeInstructions:
1. In the one gallon jar, combine the pineapple, 1 cup sugar and 2 tablespoons of brandy. Let ingredients sit in jar for 2 weeks, stirring daily.
2. At the end of two weeks, add the maraschino cherries (including juice), 1 cup sugar and 2 tablespoons of brandy. Let the mixture sit for another two weeks, stirring daily.
3. During this fourth week, separate the liquid from the fruit. The liquid is your starter and can be used to prepare cakes or ice cream later.
4. For each cake, use 1 1/2 cups of the starter, 2 1/2 cups sugar, and a large can (28 oz.) of sliced peaches. Mix well and cover jar with a paper towel. Do not refrigerate or screw lid on jar; stir every day for 10 days.
5. Add 2 1/2 cups sugar and 1 can (16 oz.) crushed pineapple. Stir daily for 10 days.
6. Add 2 1/2 cups sugar and 1 can (16 oz.) fruit cocktail. Slice contents of 1 jar (10 oz.) maraschino cherries and add with juice. Stir every day for 10 days.
7. On baking day, drain the fruit and divide it into two equal parts. Save the juice as starter for friends. You will have enough starter for five batches (approximately 2 cups each).
8. Do not refrigerate the liquid because it will stop the fermenting action.
9. For each cake, mix the cake mix, instant pudding mix, oil, and eggs until smooth. Fold in fruit and nuts. Pour mixture into a greased tube pan and bake at 350 degrees for 50 to 60 minutes. Turn out of pan while hot; when cooled, the cake freezes well.
10. Choose flavored cake mixes and pudding mixes as desired; coconut may be substituted for nuts.
